title: Dryopteris vieillardii
taxonomy: Dryopteris vieillardii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which is a group of wood ferns. The classification of Dryopteris vieillardii can be broken down further into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This fern is native to New Caledonia, an island country in the Pacific Ocean. Its specific classification is a subject of ongoing research and may be refined as new data becomes available.

intro: Dryopteris vieillardii, also known as Vieillard's shield fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris vieillardii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dryopteris vieillardii include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules for its medicinal benefits.
